title: Native GC rooting audit — migrate remaining sites to rooted scopes
purpose: SUPERSEDED PRESCRIPTION (2026-06-10): the originally prescribed pattern — save args to x-registers before ensure_heap_space and re-read after — is UNSAFE and must not be used. GC only traces x[0..live_x] where live_x is the BIF arity at dispatch, so any term saved to a register at or above the arity is NOT a root and still corrupts under heap pressure (demonstrated with lists:flatten on boxed floats). The structural mechanism landed instead: (1) GC-triggering allocators (alloc_tuple, alloc_list, alloc_list_with_tail, alloc_map, alloc_cons) now root their own term arguments internally, so single-allocation BIFs are safe with no caller action; (2) BIFs holding terms across MULTIPLE allocations or accumulating in loops must use ProcessContext::with_rooted / rooted_push / rooted, which registers terms on the process native-root stack that every collection traces and forwards; (3) NativeContinuation state is now traced via for_each_term/for_each_term_mut — any new continuation variant must implement both (the exhaustive match enforces this at compile time). Remaining work for this brief: audit BIFs not yet converted that still use the x-register save convention WITHIN arity (currently correct but deprecated) and migrate them to rooted scopes for uniformity; verify no new BIF holds heap terms in Rust locals across allocating calls without rooting.
task: Audit every BIF that holds heap-term references in Rust locals across allocating calls. Single allocations taking the terms as direct arguments are safe (self-rooting allocators). Multi-allocation sequences and accumulation loops must use context.with_rooted(...) and re-read via context.rooted(handle, i) after any potentially-collecting call, pushing loop accumulators with context.rooted_push. NEVER use the x-register save pattern: registers at or above the BIF arity are not GC roots. See crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/gc_rooting_tests.rs for the regression harness and crates/beamr/src/native/context.rs (with_rooted) for the API.

requirements.title: Audit all stdlib_stubs BIFs for unrooted terms across allocations
requirements.spec: Audit every function in crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/ that takes &[Term] args and performs more than one allocating call (or allocates in a loop) while holding heap terms in Rust locals. Convert each to context.with_rooted scopes with rooted/rooted_push. Single direct allocator calls need no change. Remove remaining x-register save sites as they are encountered.
acceptance: No BIF saves heap-term args to x-registers as a GC-rooting mechanism
Every multi-allocation BIF in stdlib_stubs uses with_rooted scopes
gc_rooting_tests pass under forced heap pressure

requirements.title: Audit gate1/gate2/gate3 BIFs
requirements.spec: Audit BIFs in bifs.rs, process_bifs.rs, and gate3_bifs/ for the same pattern. Arithmetic BIFs that allocate BigInt (after B-173) are especially important — they receive numeric args that may be heap-allocated BigInts.
acceptance: Every BIF in bifs.rs that allocates saves heap-term args first
Every BIF in process_bifs.rs that allocates saves heap-term args first
Every BIF in gate3_bifs/ that allocates saves heap-term args first
No BIF holds a heap-term Rust local across an allocation call

requirements.title: Audit OTP stubs and Gleam FFI BIFs
requirements.spec: Audit BIFs in otp_stubs/ and gleam_ffi.rs for the same pattern. These BIFs often receive complex terms (tuples, lists, binaries) and allocate result terms.
acceptance: Every BIF in otp_stubs/ that allocates saves heap-term args first
Every BIF in gleam_ffi.rs that allocates saves heap-term args first
Every BIF in selector_ffi.rs that allocates saves heap-term args first

requirements.title: Document the GC-safe BIF pattern
requirements.spec: Add a module-level doc comment to crates/beamr/src/native/mod.rs documenting the x-register save pattern for BIF authors. Include: when it's needed (any BIF that holds heap-term args and allocates), how to do it (save to x-regs, ensure_heap_space, re-read), and the reference implementation (bif_lists_reverse in collection_bifs.rs).
acceptance: Module doc in native/mod.rs describes the GC-safe BIF pattern
Pattern includes save-to-x-regs, ensure_heap_space, re-read sequence
References bif_lists_reverse as the canonical example

boundaries: BIFs that only work with small_int or atom args (immediate values, not heap pointers) do not need x-register saves
BIFs that never allocate (pure reads, comparisons) do not need x-register saves
The audit covers existing BIFs only — future BIFs follow the documented pattern
OUT OF SCOPE: Automated compile-time enforcement of the pattern (would require a custom lint)
OUT OF SCOPE: Changing the BIF calling convention to auto-save args
verification: grep -n 'ensure_heap_space' across all BIF files confirms every call is preceded by x-register saves for heap-term args
No BIF function holds heap-term locals (list, tuple, map, binary, closure Terms) across allocation calls
